# Copilot Instructions

This repository is an [Awesome List](https://github.com/sindresorhus/awesome) for the Microsoft Agent Framework. The primary file is `README.md`, which contains curated links, resources, and documentation organized into specific sections.

## Repository Structure

- `README.md` — The main content file containing all curated links and resources
- `LICENSE` — CC0 License for this repository
- `.github/agents/` — Custom agent definitions

## README.md Sections

The README.md contains the following main sections where links can be added:

- 🚀 Getting Started — Installation, quick start resources
- 📚 Official Documentation — Official docs, guides, migration guides
- 🎥 Video Resources — Video tutorials, demos, presentations
- 📖 Blog Posts & Articles — Official and community blogs, key concepts
- 🔧 Tutorials — Hands-on tutorials and guides
- 🏢 Enterprise & Production — Production features, hosting options
- 🧪 Examples & Samples — Python and .NET sample code
- 🛠️ Tools & Frameworks — Development tools, integration options
- 📊 Monitoring & Observability — Telemetry, monitoring tools
- 🔗 Related Technologies — Microsoft AI ecosystem, standards & protocols
- 👥 Community — Community resources and discussions

## Guidelines for Editing README.md

When adding or editing links and resources in README.md:

1. Analyze the link and its content type
2. Determine the most appropriate section based on the content
3. Add the link to the correct section in README.md
4. Follow the existing format and markdown style
5. Maintain alphabetical or logical ordering within sections when applicable
6. Ensure the link description is clear and informative
7. Always preserve the existing structure and formatting
8. Use the same markdown style as existing entries
9. Add links in a way that maintains the flow and organization
10. For blog posts, distinguish between "Official Microsoft Blogs" and "Community Blogs"
11. For samples, distinguish between "Python Samples" and ".NET Samples"
12. Keep descriptions concise but informative
13. Use proper markdown formatting for links: `[Link Text](URL) - Description`

## What NOT to Do

- Do not remove existing content unless explicitly asked
- Do not change the overall structure of the README
- Do not modify sections unrelated to the requested changes
- Do not add links to irrelevant sections
- Do not change the emoji icons or section headers

## Code Review Guidelines

When reviewing pull requests that modify README.md:

- Verify that new links are placed in the correct section
- Check that the markdown format matches existing entries: `[Link Text](URL) - Description`
- Ensure blog posts are categorized correctly (Official Microsoft Blogs vs Community Blogs)
- Ensure samples are categorized correctly (Python Samples vs .NET Samples)
- Confirm that the existing structure, formatting, and section headers are preserved
- Confirm that no existing content was removed unintentionally
- Verify descriptions are concise but informative
- Check that alphabetical or logical ordering is maintained within sections
